section FiniteBiproducts

variable {J : Type} [Finite J] {K : Type} [Finite K] {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
  [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasFiniteBiproducts C] {f : J → C} {g : K → C}

/-- Convert a (dependently typed) matrix to a morphism of biproducts. -/
def biproduct.matrix (m : ∀ j k, f j ⟶ g k) : ⨁ f ⟶ ⨁ g :=
  biproduct.desc fun j => biproduct.lift fun k => m j k

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem biproduct.matrix_π (m : ∀ j k, f j ⟶ g k) (k : K) :
    biproduct.matrix m ≫ biproduct.π g k = biproduct.desc fun j => m j k := by
  ext
  simp [biproduct.matrix]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem biproduct.ι_matrix (m : ∀ j k, f j ⟶ g k) (j : J) :
    biproduct.ι f j ≫ biproduct.matrix m = biproduct.lift fun k => m j k := by
  simp [biproduct.matrix]

/-- Extract the matrix components from a morphism of biproducts. -/
def biproduct.components (m : ⨁ f ⟶ ⨁ g) (j : J) (k : K) : f j ⟶ g k :=
  biproduct.ι f j ≫ m ≫ biproduct.π g k

@[simp]
theorem biproduct.matrix_components (m : ∀ j k, f j ⟶ g k) (j : J) (k : K) :
    biproduct.components (biproduct.matrix m) j k = m j k := by simp [biproduct.components]

@[simp]
theorem biproduct.components_matrix (m : ⨁ f ⟶ ⨁ g) :
    (biproduct.matrix fun j k => biproduct.components m j k) = m := by
  ext
  simp [biproduct.components]

/-- Morphisms between direct sums are matrices. -/
@[simps]
def biproduct.matrixEquiv : (⨁ f ⟶ ⨁ g) ≃ ∀ j k, f j ⟶ g k where
  toFun := biproduct.components
  invFun := biproduct.matrix
  left_inv := biproduct.components_matrix
  right_inv m := by

545	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
546	
547	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
548	
549	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
550	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
551	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
552	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
553	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
554	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
555	
556	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
557	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
558	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
559	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
560	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
561	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
562	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
563	
564	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
565	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
566	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
567	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
568	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
569	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
570	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
571	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
572	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
573	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
574	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
575	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
576	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
577	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
578	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
579	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
580	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
581	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
582	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
583	
584	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
585	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
586	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
587	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
588	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
589	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
590	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
591	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
592	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
593	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
594	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
595	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
596	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
597	    matrixToFreeIso
598	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
599	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
600	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
601	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
602	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
603	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
604	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
605	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
606	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
607	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
608	
609	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
610	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
611	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
612	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
613	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
614	
615	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
616	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
617	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
618	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
619	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
620	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
621	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
622	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
623	`chartTransition`. -/
624	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
625	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
626	      = eqToIso (congrArg
627	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
628	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
629	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
630	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
631	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
632	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
633	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
634	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
635	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
636	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
637	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
638	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
639	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
640	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
641	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
642	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
643	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
644	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
645	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
646	      = Iso.refl _ := by
647	    apply Iso.ext
648	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
649	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
650	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
651	  simp only [bundleTransition]
652	  erw [hB, Iso.refl_trans]
653	  exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso hφ (Fin d)
654	
655	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
656	gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
657	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
658	    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
659	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj
660	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
661	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)).obj
662	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
663	  fun I J => bundleTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
664	
665	/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)
666	
667	The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
668	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
669	Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
670	`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
671	are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
672	helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/
673	
674	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.mul_submatrix_col` (private there). -/
675	private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
676	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
677	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
678	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
679	
680	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_nonsing_inv` (private there). -/
681	private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
682	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
683	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
684	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
685	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
686	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
687	
688	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_map_eq_of_comp` (private there). -/
689	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp' {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*}
690	    [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
691	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
692	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
693	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]
694	
695	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.isUnit_algebraMap_away_left` (private there). -/
696	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
697	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
698	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
699	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
700	  rw [map_mul] at h
701	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
702	
703	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel` (private there). -/
704	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
705	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
706	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
707	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
708	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]
709	
710	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.imageMatrix_map_eq` (private there). -/
711	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
712	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
713	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
714	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
715	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
716	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
717	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
718	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
719	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
720	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
721	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
722	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
723	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
724	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
725	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
726	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
727	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
728	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
729	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
730	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
731	    ← map_nonsing_inv' incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
732	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
733	  congr 1
734	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]
735	
736	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` (private there): over the triple
737	overlap `S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied
738	entrywise to the image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. -/
739	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
740	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
741	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
742	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
743	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
744	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
745	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
746	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I K hI hK

748	    (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)

752	  set Y := (universalMatrix d r I hI).map

754	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hY
755	  have hYJ : IsUnit (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det := by
756	    have e : (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
757	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)

768	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
769	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
770	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
771	  have hMJ : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
772	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
773	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
774	      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
775	    have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
776	          ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
777	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
778	        = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
779	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
780	      rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
781	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
782	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
783	      congr 1
784	      exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
785	    rw [e1, hMJimg]
786	  have hRHS : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
787	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
788	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
789	      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
790	    have hmm : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
791	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
792	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
793	        = (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
794	            ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
795	              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
796	          * ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
797	              (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
798	                (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).map
799	                  ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
800	                    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
801	      rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
802	    rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, hMJ, universalMinorInv,
803	      ← map_nonsing_inv' _ _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r J K hJ hK), universalMinor,
804	      map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, hMJ,
805	      mul_submatrix_col' (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ Y
806	        (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)),
807	      Matrix.mul_inv_rev, Matrix.nonsing_inv_nonsing_inv _ hYJ,
808	      inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' _ _ _ hYJ]
809	  rw [hLHS, hRHS]

811	/-- **Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap (L1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix`):
812	over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` the base-changed Cramer inverses of
813	the localised minors satisfy the multiplicative cocycle identity
814	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹`. This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent
815	of any sheaf data. Project-local. -/
816	theorem bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
817	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
818	    (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
819	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
820	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
821	      * (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
822	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
823	      = (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
824	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
825	  -- Take the `I`-minor (columns indexed by `I`) of the image-matrix cocycle.
826	  have hcol := congrArg
827	    (fun M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away
828	        (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) =>
829	      M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)))
830	    (cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' d r I J K hI hJ hK)
831	  simp only at hcol
832	  -- LHS of `hcol` is `(X^I_K)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
833	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I] at hcol
834	  -- RHS of `hcol`: push the `I`-minor through the outer map.
835	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map] at hcol
836	  -- `imageMatrix J K = (X^J_K)⁻¹ * X^J`, so its `I`-minor splits off the inverse factor;
837	  -- the second factor is `X^J` (over `R^J[1/P^J_K]`) restricted to the `I`-columns.
838	  have hsplit : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).submatrix id
839	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
840	      = universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK *
841	        ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
842	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
843	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
844	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) := by
845	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact mul_submatrix_col' _ _ _
846	  rw [hsplit, Matrix.map_mul] at hcol
847	  -- The comp identity `θ_{I,J}` realises the cross-localisation map on `R^J`.
848	  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
849	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
850	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
851	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
852	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
853	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
854	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
855	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
856	  have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
857	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
858	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
859	      = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
860	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
861	    rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
862	        (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
863	        (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
864	    congr 1
865	    exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
866	  -- The base change of `X^J|_I` over `θ_{I,J}` is `(X^I_J)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
867	  have hXJI : (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
868	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
869	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
870	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
871	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
872	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
873	      = (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
874	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
875	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, e1,
876	      Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
877	  rw [hXJI] at hcol
878	  exact hcol.symm

880	/-! ### L3 transport: `scalarEnd`/`matrixEnd` naturality under pullback
881	
882	The substantive new infrastructure for (C2). The atom is `scalarEnd_pullback`: pulling back a
883	scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` along a scheme morphism `p` is, after the unit-pullback
884	comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the base-changed function

200	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
201	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
202	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
203	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
204	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
205	  hom := matrixEnd M
206	  inv := matrixEnd N
207	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
208	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]
209	
210	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
211	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
212	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl
213	
214	/-- **Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively** (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`): the
215	forward maps of two matrix automorphisms compose to `matrixEnd` of the matrix product (with
216	the order reversed by the column/component contravariance). This is the linkage that turns
217	the matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module
218	isomorphisms. Project-local. -/
219	lemma matrixToFreeIso_mul {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
220	    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
221	    (hAA' : A * A' = 1) (hA'A : A' * A = 1) (hBB' : B * B' = 1) (hB'B : B' * B = 1) :
222	    (matrixToFreeIso A A' hAA' hA'A).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' hBB' hB'B).hom
223	      = matrixEnd (B * A) := by
224	  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]
225	
226	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
227	
228	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
229	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
230	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
231	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
232	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
233	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
234	
235	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
236	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
237	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
238	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
239	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
240	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
241	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
242	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
243	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
244	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
245	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
246	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
247	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
248	
249	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
250	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
251	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/

326	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
327	
328	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
329	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
330	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
331	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
332	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
333	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
334	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
335	
336	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
337	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
338	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
339	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
340	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
341	
342	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
343	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
344	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
345	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
346	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
347	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
348	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
349	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
350	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
351	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
352	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
353	
354	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
355	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
356	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
357	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
358	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
359	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
360	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
361	
362	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
363	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
364	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
365	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
366	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
367	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
368	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
369	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
370	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
371	    Category.assoc]
372	
373	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
374	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
375	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
376	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
377	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
378	the module cocycle (C2). -/
379	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
380	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
381	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
382	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
383	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
384	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
385	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
386	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
387	
388	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
389	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
390	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
391	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
392	
393	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
394	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
395	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
396	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
397	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
398	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
399	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
400	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
401	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
402	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
403	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
404	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
405	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
406	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
407	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed

input.new_string:
`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction.

Construction (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): the glued sheaf is built directly as a
categorical limit — an **equalizer of pushforwards** — rather than a hand-built presheaf of
compatible families. Concretely, `glue` forms the two parallel maps
`∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{i,j} (ιᵢ ≫ f_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` (one leg the adjunction unit composed
with the pushforward-composition comparison, the other transported across the inverse
transition `(g_ij)⁻¹`), and takes their equalizer inside `Scheme.Modules D.glued`. The
self-identity (C1) and triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) hypotheses `_hC1`/`_hC2` on the
family `g` are NOT consumed in forming the equalizer object (the limit exists for any family
of transition maps); they are the descent conditions pinned down downstream when the
restriction isomorphisms are produced. The body below and the `_hC1`/`_hC2` signature are
complete (axiom-clean since iter-056). -/

/-- **Transport of a matrix automorphism through `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the
reusable (a)→(c) bridge for the bundle cocycle (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_transport`). A
transition isomorphism of the bundle-transition shape
`pullbackFreeIso a ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso b).symm` (a `GL_d` matrix
automorphism conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along `p : W ⟶ V` to the same
shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, with the matrix base-changed entrywise by the comorphism
`p.appTop`. Combines the matrix-naturality atom `matrixEnd_pullback` with the free-pullback
pseudofunctor coherence `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp`. Project-local — this is the
abstract core of the bundle cocycle transport, independent of the Grassmannian charts. -/
lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) {d : ℕ}
    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(V, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).symm)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom ≫
        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
  sorry

  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the
  -- composite free-pullback comparison `Q_{p≫a}` (pseudofunctoriality, `pullbackFreeIso_comp`).
  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d)).hom ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom := by
    rw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d), ← Category.assoc,
      Iso.symm_hom, ← NatTrans.comp_app, Iso.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.id_app, Category.id_comp]
  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into the
  -- inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`.
  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).inv ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
    rw [← Functor.map_inv_eq_inv]
    sorry
  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated matrix automorphism and apply the matrix atom.
  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, matrixEnd_pullback]

content:
.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/NatIso.lean:53:def app {F G : C ⥤ D} (α : F ≅ G) (X : C) :
===

/-- The application of a natural isomorphism to an object. We put this definition in a different
namespace, so that we can use `α.app` -/
@[simps (attr := grind =)]
def app {F G : C ⥤ D} (α : F ≅ G) (X : C) :
    F.obj X ≅ G.obj X where
  hom := α.hom.app X
  inv := α.inv.app X

set_option linter.existingAttributeWarning false in
attribute [to_dual existing app_inv] app_hom

@[reassoc +to_dual (attr := simp), grind =]
theorem hom_inv_id_app {F G : C ⥤ D} (α : F ≅ G) (X : C) :
    α.hom.app X ≫ α.inv.app X = 𝟙 (F.obj X) := by cat_disch

@[reassoc +to_dual (attr := simp), grind =]
theorem inv_hom_id_app {F G : C ⥤ D} (α : F ≅ G) (X : C) :
    α.inv.app X ≫ α.hom.app X = 𝟙 (G.obj X) := by cat_disch

@[reassoc +to_dual (attr := simp)]
lemma hom_inv_id_app_app {F G : C ⥤ D ⥤ E} (e : F ≅ G) (X₁ : C) (X₂ : D) :
    (e.hom.app X₁).app X₂ ≫ (e.inv.app X₁).app X₂ = 𝟙 _ := by cat_disch

@[reassoc +to_dual (attr := simp)]
lemma inv_hom_id_app_app {F G : C ⥤ D ⥤ E} (e : F ≅ G) (X₁ : C) (X₂ : D) :
    (e.inv.app X₁).app X₂ ≫ (e.hom.app X₁).app X₂ = 𝟙 _ := by cat_disch

@[reassoc +to_dual (attr := simp)]
lemma hom_inv_id_app_app_app {F G : C ⥤ D ⥤ E ⥤ E'} (e : F ≅ G)
    (X₁ : C) (X₂ : D) (X₃ : E) :
    ((e.hom.app X₁).app X₂).app X₃ ≫ ((e.inv.app X₁).app X₂).app X₃ = 𝟙 _ := by cat_disch

@[reassoc +to_dual (attr := simp)]
lemma inv_hom_id_app_app_app {F G : C ⥤ D ⥤ E ⥤ E'} (e : F ≅ G)
    (X₁ : C) (X₂ : D) (X₃ : E) :
    ((e.inv.app X₁).app X₂).app X₃ ≫ ((e.hom.app X₁).app X₂).app X₃ = 𝟙 _ := by cat_disch

end Iso

input.old_string:
have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d)).hom ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom := by
    rw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d), ← Category.assoc,
      Iso.symm_hom, ← NatTrans.comp_app, Iso.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.id_app, Category.id_comp]
  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into the
  -- inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`.
  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).inv ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
    rw [← Functor.map_inv_eq_inv]
    sorry
  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated matrix automorphism and apply the matrix atom.
  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, matrixEnd_pullback]
  sorry
input.new_string:
have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d)).hom ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom := by
    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d)]
    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
    rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]
  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into the
  -- inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`. Derived by inverting the `b`-leg coherence iso.
  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).inv ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
    have hiso : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)) ≪≫
          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)
        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)) ≪≫
          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d) := by
      apply Iso.ext
      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp b p (Fin d)
    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv
    -- hinv : Q_{p≫b}.inv ≫ Cpb.inv.app free = Q_p.inv ≫ (pullback p).map Q_b.inv
    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Category.assoc, Iso.app_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id_app, Category.comp_id]
  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated matrix automorphism and apply the matrix atom.
  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, matrixEnd_pullback]
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  rw [reassoc_of% hfront, hback]

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean — iter-063 prover

## Summary

- **Sorry count: 5 → 4.**
- **Closed:** `matrixEnd_pullback` (the assigned headline step **(a)**, `lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback`).
- **New fully-proven lemmas added (0 sorry, axiom-clean):**
  - `ιFree_matrixEnd` — `ιFree j ≫ matrixEnd M = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k` (helper for (a)).
  - `pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso` — the **abstract (a)→(c) bridge**: transports a
    bundle-transition-shaped iso `pullbackFreeIso a ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso b).symm`
    through `pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b` to `pullbackFreeIso (p≫a) ≪≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop·M) ≪≫
    (pullbackFreeIso (p≫b)).inv`. This is step **(c)'s reusable core** (independent of the Grassmannian
    charts), the planner's `bundleTransition_cocycle_transport` engine.
- **Still open (4):** `bundleTransition_cocycle` (C2, L1045), `universalQuotient` (L1118),
  `tautologicalQuotient` (L1128), `represents` (L1677). C2 gates the three riders.
- **Hygiene:** rewrote the stale section NOTE at L311–327 (the `glue` doc) to describe the actual
  equalizer-of-pushforwards construction (lean-auditor recurring MAJOR, now fixed).
- **Adjacent sorries attempted beyond assigned (a):** yes — built the full (c) abstract core lemma.
- **Build:** real `lake build` green, 29s, no kernel timeout / OOM (8319 jobs).

## matrixEnd_pullback (step a) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean

### Approach (worked)
- New helper `ιFree_matrixEnd`: `rw [matrixEnd, ιFree]; simp only [free]`, then
  `biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv/_hom`, `Sigma.ι_desc` (as a `have`, then `← Category.assoc`),
  `biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc`, `biproduct.lift_desc`, `rfl`.
- Main proof: cancel `Q.inv` via `← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv`; then
  `Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback p) _ _ (isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)))`
  reduces to one free injection `i`. `simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]`.
  Key helper `key : (pullback p).map (ιFree k) ≫ Q.hom = pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree k` from Mathlib's
  `pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom` (after `hQhom : Q.hom = (pullbackObjFreeIso …).hom := rfl`,
  needs `haveI := opensMap_final p`). Then distribute over the row sum (`Functor.map_sum` — pullback is
  additive, found by instance), and reduce each entry to the proven atom `scalarEnd_pullback` via
  `reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)`.

### CRITICAL TOOLING LESSON (the recurring `free`/`∐` value-diamond)
`rw`/`simp` repeatedly **fail to match `ιFree _ ≫ matrixEnd _`, `Functor.map_comp`, `Functor.map_sum`,
`Preadditive.comp_sum`, `Category.assoc` when those subterms sit under `(pullback p).map (…)` or involve
`free (Fin d)` (= `∐ unit`) objects** — the displayed term is identical but elaborated with a defeq-but-not-
syntactic object form. **`erw` (defeq matching) bridges all of them.** This is the same diamond as
`grquot-functor-dropped-termmode`. Concretely the pattern that worked:
`erw [ιFree_matrixEnd M i]`, `erw [Functor.map_sum]`, `erw [Category.assoc]`, `erw [Functor.map_comp]`,
`erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]`, `erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback …]`.
**DANGER:** `erw [Category.assoc]` when the leftmost factor is `pullbackComp.app.hom` **whnf's the OPAQUE
`pullbackComp` into its full leftUnitor/associator/whiskerRight pseudofunctor definition** (huge term, near-OOM).
Keep `pullbackComp` away from `erw`; use plain `rw` with explicit args or, where the two sides are
definitionally associativity-equal, `rfl` (see below).

## pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso (step c core) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean

### Approach (worked)
- `simp only [pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.symm_hom,
  matrixToFreeIso_hom]` exposes
  `Cpa.symm.app(free).hom ≫ (pullback p).map (Qa.hom ≫ matrixEnd M ≫ Qb.inv) ≫ Cpb.app(free).hom`.
- **hfront** `Cpa.symm.app.hom ≫ (pullback p).map Qa.hom ≫ Qp.hom = Q_{p≫a}.hom`:
  `erw [← pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d)]; simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom];
   rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]`.  (`pullbackFreeIso_comp` already proven, L~1487.)
- **hback** `Qp.inv ≫ (pullback p).map Qb.inv ≫ Cpb.app.hom = Q_{p≫b}.inv`: build the `b`-leg coherence
  as an **iso** equation `hiso` (`Iso.ext` + `simpa using pullbackFreeIso_comp b p`), invert with
  `congrArg Iso.inv` + `simp [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv]`, then
  `rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]; erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]; rw [Category.comp_id]`.
- Assembly: `rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, matrixEnd_pullback]` (these 3 match with plain `rw`!),
  then `rw [← hfront, ← hback]; rfl` — **the residual is pure categorical associativity which is
  *definitional* here, so `rfl` closes it.** (Every `rw/simp/erw [Category.assoc]` was obstructed —
  diamond / pullbackComp-whnf / slice mis-index — but `rfl` after the two coherence-expansions works.)

### KEY REUSABLE FINDINGS
- `pullbackFreeIso_comp (a) (p)` (note arg order: lemma is `(a : Ty⟶Tx) (b : Tz⟶Ty)`, so pass
  `a` then `p` to get `pullbackComp p a` / `p ≫ a`).
- `rw [← hfront, ← hback]; rfl` pattern: when both sides are equal up to associativity of a long
  composite over `SheafOfModules`, `rfl` succeeds where `Category.assoc` rewriting fails.

## bundleTransition_cocycle (C2) — STILL OPEN; precise remaining work

The abstract transport core (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso`) is now proven, so each of the
three `pullbackBaseChangeTransport (bundleTransition …)` legs reduces to `matrixEnd` of a base-changed
Cramer inverse over the triple overlap. **Two blockers remain:**

1. **Ordering.** `pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso` sits at L1543 (it needs
   `pullbackFreeIso_comp`, L~1487), but C2 is at L1045. To use it in C2, a future iter must relocate the
   `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` + `pullbackFreeIso_comp` cluster (and the new transport lemma) **before**
   C2. Nothing between L1045 and L1530 references `bundleTransition_cocycle` (the riders are `sorry`), so
   the move is safe; none of these decls are protected (`archon-protected.yaml` lists none from this file).
   Alternatively move C2 + riders to after the cluster.
2. **Base-change bridge (b)** — still genuinely missing. After applying the transport lemma, each leg is
   `matrixEnd ((projection/t').appTop.mapMatrix (ΓSpecIso.inv·(X^I_J)⁻¹))`. To feed L1
   (`bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`, over `cocycleΘIJ`/`awayInclLeft`/`awayInclRight`) one must identify
   `(pullback.fst …).appTop ∘ ΓSpecIso.inv` (and the `t'` version) with `awayInclLeft/Right`/`cocycleΘIJ`.
   This needs the **GrassmannianCells internals** (how `theGlueData.f/.t/.t'` are built from
   `chartIncl/chartTransition/transitionMap` and the localisation structure) — NOT in this file's scope.
   This is the `lem:gr_baseChange_bridge` the planner forward-pinned; it is the true remaining crux.

## Why I stopped — `Real progress`

Closed 1 assigned sorry (`matrixEnd_pullback`, the iter-063 headline (a)), axiom-clean and validated by a
real `lake build`. Added **two** net-new fully-proven, axiom-clean lemmas (`ιFree_matrixEnd`,
`pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso` = step (c)'s abstract core). Fixed the recurring stale `glue`
section NOTE. Sorry count 5→4.

C2 is not closed for two concrete, named reasons: (1) a declaration-ordering move is needed to bring the
proven transport core before C2 (mechanical, safe, deferred to avoid risky large surgery mid-session), and
(2) the base-change bridge `lem:gr_baseChange_bridge` genuinely requires GrassmannianCells internals
(`theGlueData`/`chartTransition` ↔ `awayIncl`/`cocycleΘIJ` via ΓSpecIso naturality) that live outside this
file. The universalQuotient/tautologicalQuotient/represents riders all gate on C2 and were not attempted.

## Blueprint flags for review/plan agent
- `lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback` (matrixEnd_pullback) → now **\leanok-ready** (proof closed).
- `pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso` and `ιFree_matrixEnd` are **new project-local lemmas with
  no blueprint env yet** — plan agent should add `lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_transport` (referenced in the
  lemma docstring) and a helper block, then they can be marked.

If you can see a direct path to any of them using lemmas or infrastructure you've already built, attempt them. Stopping at the assigned task boundary when adjacent work is tractable is artificial throttling — you have write permission over your whole file.
- **Comments are not progress. Code is.** If you can describe an approach precisely enough to write it as a comment or TODO, you can attempt it. Any `-- TODO: try X`, `-- could use Y here`, or `/- Next step: ... -/` you write is a sign you stopped too early — attempt X/Y first; only leave the comment if the attempt fails with a named error. The same applies to approaches proposed in task results: if you wrote it in your log as "a possible route", you should have tried it.
- **Autonomous decision-making within your file.** You are fully authorized to make every tactical and mathematical decision within your assigned `.lean` file without waiting for the planner. This includes: choosing proof strategies, adding helper lemmas, attempting adjacent sorries, selecting alternative approaches, deciding proof structure. **Never write "I'll let the planner decide", "I'll defer to the next iteration", or "this requires planner input" for any choice that is entirely within your file.** The planner is not on call; deferring wastes the entire context-loading investment of this session.

  The ONLY actions that require stopping rather than acting:
  - Modifying a *signature* of a protected declaration (you may still fill its proof body).
  - Editing another agent's `.lean` file.
  - Removing an existing public declaration that other files import.
  - Changing the type of an existing declaration in a way that would break other files.

  Everything else — including trying a strategy the planner didn't explicitly endorse — is your call to make and attempt now.

## When to stop

Stop only when progress is blocked by a specific, named obstacle in one of these categories:

1. A genuinely missing Mathlib ingredient — named precisely, attempted via informal agent, no workaround found.
2. A definition or signature that requires another prover's output — name the specific declaration and file.
3. A proof that requires fundamental restructuring of a protected or cross-file definition — name it.

"It seems hard", "the proof is complex", "I couldn't find the right lemma", or "the planner should weigh in on this approach" are NOT valid stop reasons. Document the specific obstacle and keep trying. Only stop when you can write: "I tried approaches X, Y, Z; each fails because [specific mathematical reason]; the informal agent suggested [route] which requires [specific missing ingredient]."

**Specifically banned stop phrases:**
- "I'll wait for the planner to decide."
- "This strategy should be confirmed by the next iteration."
- "I'll defer this choice to the planner."
- "The planner may want to reconsider the approach."

If you find yourself writing any of these: delete the sentence, pick the most promising route, and attempt it. The planner will correct the choice next iter if it was wrong — a failed attempt costs nothing compared to an idle session.

## Completion criteria

Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:

1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
2. Zero axioms introduced.
3. The file compiles cleanly.

## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof

When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature**. Three patterns are banned:

- **Reflexive-iso placeholder** — replacing `X ≅ Y` with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`.
- **`Classical.choice` around an explicit witness** — dissolving `Type` vs `Prop` friction without actually constructing the witness.
- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`** — discards the declaration post-elaboration, breaking blueprint cross-references.

**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, ship the typed `sorry` instead.

## When infrastructure is missing

Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before filing "Infrastructure missing":

1. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 2.
2. **If the missing ingredient is ≤~100 LOC**: write it as a project-local helper — typed signature plus a genuine proof attempt. A partial proof body is better than a documented gap.
3. **Only after steps 1–2 are exhausted**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` with what you tried, why it failed, and the precise statement of the missing ingredient for the plan agent to assign in `mathlib-build` mode.

"Infrastructure missing" as a verdict requires: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), local helper attempted if in scope. "I don't see how" is not evidence — a failed attempt with `sorry` at the stuck step IS evidence.

When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.

**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

## Proof style

- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

Before writing results:

1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles.
3. **Write a `## Summary` section** stating: sorry count before → after; exact names of sorries closed; exact names of sorries still open and why; whether you attempted adjacent sorries beyond the assigned ones.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.
   - `Infrastructure missing`: a specific Mathlib gap was the blocker — name it precisely, confirm the informal agent was called (or key unavailable), describe what it suggested, why it failed, and state the exact missing ingredient statement.
   - `Directive not followed`: explain which part of the planner's directive you deviated from and why.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic
2	import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
3	import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
4	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
5	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal
6	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification

8	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal
